Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-20-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Melbostad, Esq., counsel for Georgia Mitchell, former Trustee; Meta Mitchell and Justin Mitchell, beneficiaries (appearing by video conference); Peter Borenstein, Esq., counsel for James Mitchell, beneficiary (appearing by video conference)

At the Court's invitation, Mr. Melbostad addresses the opposition to his motion to withdraw as counsel and the concerns with respect to the informal accounting provided by Mr. Melbostad on behalf of the former Trustee, advising that due to the passage of time and intervening events, the accounting presented, such as it is, is the best that could be provided. As certain of the documents requested by opposing counsel contain confidential attorney/client communications pertaining to criminal defense matters, the documents would only be provided to the Court in camera.

Mr. Borenstein contends that the accounting, such as it is, is based entirely on hearsay, is unverified, and cannot be considered an accounting in any form. Mr. Melbostad contends that much of the information contained in the informal accounting is based on his personal knowledge and as such is not hearsay.

At the Court's query, Mr. Borenstein acknowledges that some portion of his client's attorney fees from separate litigation were paid from trust assests but the exact amount is not known at this moment.

After further discussion, the Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Dillingham. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Melbostad appeared by zoom for Georgia Mitchell.
Peter Bornstein appeared by zoom for James Mitchell (Petitioner).

Mr. Melbostad stated that he remembers that Lisa changed her mind about serving as successor trustee, that Georgia is aging, and her capacity is questionable, that the trust was never administered at his office and that it was administered at Georgia's house in Lodi. Mr. Melbostad also stated that he lost contact with Georgia about ten years ago. Mr. Bornstein noted that in 2010 the trust was abandoned and so was his client's share, and stated that Mr. Melbostad has never withdrawn, and that Mr. Melbostad is still on the hook. Mr. Melbostad stated that all of the assets were used to defend two criminal matters. The court advised Mr. Melbostad that he needs to do something in response to Judge Quinn's order. Discussion was held regarding Mr. Melbostad providing an accounting and information regarding where the assets went, whether or not Mr. Melbostad should be held liable, whether or not the accounting needs to be filed with the court, etc. Mr. Melbostad is to provide an informal accounting to Mr. Bornstein by November 8, 2021. The Petition for Breach of Trust, etc. is continued to November 22, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is also continued to that date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Off Calendar Aug-02-2021 Continued To Sep-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Melbostad, via ZOOM, for Justin Mitchell, Meta Mitchell and Georgia Mitchell, Petitioners; Peter Bornstein, via ZOOM, for James Mitchell, Petitioner.

The court begins by asking Mr. Melbostad if Lisa Mitchell is his client. Mr. Melbostad replies no and reports that Lisa was never his client. The court then reminds Mr. Melbostad that the court granted Mr. Bornstein's petition to compel accounting one year ago and that he has failed to comply. Mr. Melbostad reports that the order issued in this matter on 8/03/2010, discharged his clients and appointed Lisa Mitchell as successor trustee. Mr. Bornstein reports that he filed a judicial notice on 10/05/2010 to contradict Mr. Bornstein's report. The court advises Mr. Melbostad that it is requesting that he comply with the court's order dated 7/31/2020 which granted Mr. Bornstein's petition to compel accounting. Mr. Melobstad reports that he does not have access to records to begin assembling the accounting and once again reports that he currently does not represent any party in this matter. Regardless, the court instructs Mr. Melbostad to prepare and file an accounting in accordance with the court's 7/31/2020 order. The court notes that Lisa Mitchell had been previously ordered to appear today. Accordingly, the Order to Appear as to her specifically will be continued to the next calling of this matter.

The Petition for Breach of Trust, etc., and Order of Status Hearing (Order to Appear) are continued to are continued to September 20, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Ms. Deshmukh stated that Mr. Melbostad was to have filed the accounting in September, that the terms of the prior stipulation were to remain in effect, and that he had added language to the stipulation. Mr. Melbostad stated that he filed the stipulation with new language regarding setting up an annual meeting with the shareholders. The Court ordered today's status hearing off calendar and stated that it would see the parties on November 16, 2009.

Mr. Melbostad stated that the Parties needed to agree on a date for the Share Holders and Directors for the Cinema 7, Inc. to meet and conduct their annual meeting, and that he opposed the petition for instructions. Ms. Deshmukh stated that she wanted notice of action and that she wanted the option to participate. The matters were continued to November 16, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that continuation is pursuant to the same terms of the stipulation in place. With regard to the removal petition, the Court stated that it would like to start with a formal accounting. Status of said accounting is set for October 5, 2009. Mr. Melbostad provided Ms. Deshmukh with a complete set of bank statements in open court. The Court stated that the sub trusts needed to be funded. Mr. Melbostad referred to paragraph 8.8 of the trust, explained that four shares were funded, that the trust required division of the assets into four shares, that separate accounts were set up for each of the Children and that the separate shares have been funded. Ms. Deshmukh stated that four trusts had not been established and that there were no separate tax identification numbers. Mr. Melbostad will talk to Ms. Deshmukh with regard to this issue and prepare a stipulation.
